owasp_category: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
title: Restore Audit Logging and Alerting
objective: Restore audit logging and enable alerting for large data transfers.
briefing: A large backup file was downloaded and only showed up in the plain access log - the audit-logging/alerting service that should have flagged it is stopped.
hints: Restarting apache2 isn't the same as starting the audit/alerting service.
Starting the service alone isn't enough either - it also needs alerting turned on.
debrief: Starting the audit-logging service with alerting enabled means future anomalous events - like a multi-megabyte download of a sensitive file - get flagged in real time instead of quietly blending into ordinary access logs. Starting the service without turning alerting on is a half-measure: logs get written again, but nothing is watching them, which is the exact gap that caused the original incident. This addresses Security Logging and Monitoring Failures (A09:2021) directly: monitoring has to be running and configured with rules for what 'suspicious' looks like, not just technically capable of logging.

logs: [WARN] audit-logging service stopped - no security events will be recorded

cases.output:
starting audit-logging service...
alerting rules loaded: large-transfer, off-hours-access, repeated-auth-failure
done

logs_append: [FIX] audit logging restarted - alerting enabled for anomalous transfers

cases.output:
starting audit-logging service...
no alerting rules loaded - service is running but nothing will flag anomalies
done

logs_append: [PARTIAL] audit-logging started without alerting - still not monitoring for anomalies
